GN[9x9 ladder challenge: cook777 vs keiler]
C[Uberdude: Leaves the top open, but L17 would be too flat with e16 press.
Uberdude: Another idea would be L14, I didn't jump out at m14 which would be a safe move. It is nice for white to get o5 in sente to guard against m3.
C[Uberdude: A nice idea, q12 more normal but helps me settle.
C[Rathbone: Would S10 be any better for making life?
C[Rathbone: playing ko seems expensive for white given the lack of ko threats, could white have tried to live at s10 instead?

C[Uberdude: Right decision I think, right side is huge and thick for you now, though r3 still open. I thought you might answer and use p17 as your ko threat. I can't really answer as then you have more threats, so I take the ko which is huge, but p17 + p18 is big too, makes sure r17 is very alive (weaker after I win ko) and o17 group is then weak and needs to jump out at m14 so you come out too at k14.
C[Uberdude: Really surprised me, seems risky to make more groups.
Rathbone: right, so maybe H4 instead, but still 2 weak groups in any case?
C[Uberdude: Maybe I should just take the points at d2 and let you get e5 d4 f6.
C[Uberdude: Maybe this is too much.

C[Uberdude: I could back down and squeeze at g6, but as I lost the c7 liberty I need to come back and to defend c4. That should still be ok for white as I solidified the entire lower side and corner, and in exchange you made e8 stronger and can come back to attacking d10 and helping your group there.
C[Uberdude: Good tesuji, I missed this cut to gain you speed. I was only thinking of d2 which gives me time to h5, and then come back to b4, and h5 means I can answer h4 at h2.
C[Uberdude: Lower group is thin now.
C[Uberdude: A nice idea to sacrifice and build the centre.

C[Uberdude: A solid move, and obviously my push at f7 aims at this cut, but sente is huge! If you can seal a big centre with m14 you have good prospects as the h8 cut is a mess but stilla  fight. I have bad aji with black f13 (I can't atari at g13 or else b12 b10 d13 d14 c14) and even after e14 connect there is still the aji of e15 push and cut so you can likely get some moves on the outside in sente which will help the h8 fight.
C[Uberdude: slow, I can't really kill that group. Either q2/p3, or m12 is still big, or something more agressive like m3. Or get the moves in sente around m6 like in game then m12.
C[Uberdude: I really wanted to take more points at r3, but that hurts o3 too much with h4 so strong.
C[Uberdude: I am really hoping you answer this so I can get these yose _and_ m12, you should probably resist at m12.
C[Uberdude: I decided even if I m12 I could lose so played here as it's big and means your group isn't quite alive yet which gives me a chance to complicate the game.
C[Uberdude: Another soft move, you're letting me back in the game. f13 e14 g13 would be a similar idea but bigger. Or the m6 sente stuff and then m12.
C[Uberdude: Now I though I could win with excellent yose.
C[Uberdude: Now that h15 means h18 won't be anything might as well get this in (hopefully) sente.
C[Uberdude: t18 is bigger, you decided to resist at the wrong time!
C[Uberdude: This is a mistake, t16 is also sente and means I have less good 1st line yose.
C[Uberdude: If you hane I don't think I can win the ko as you are very thick everywhere.

C[Uberdude: Here I had a really nasty surprise, I think instead of s14 you can play f13 can capture me! f13 e14 b12 b10 c14 d13 e15 f15 f16 and seems you have more liberties and I can't get more thanks to h15 and h12. So h15 was sente and you could have won the game with this sequence any time in the last 20 moves.

C[Uberdude: Had to do some careful reading here that I could play this and keep L11 stones: l12 k12 l13 k13 k15 l16 l15 m17 m18 n17 n18 and then o15 is the tesuji that wins it. Instead of o15 n14 seems to die with n14 o16 p16 o15 o14 p15 p17 o13 and the 3 stones at l13 are only 1 eye and I can't connect to  f14 unless I give up f13 in response to the peep at g13. In fact if you do all that I have gained already to can give them up, so peeping at g13 before m11 as a probe looks best.
Uberdude: Althought this means you can capture L11 stones, I can capture n14 in exchange and they are about the same size, which means I got L6 tedomari (it's about 8 points).
C[Uberdude: Thanks for the game, well played. Improve your yose and you can win!

C[KyuT: I think this should be at F4 aiming at making E5 and F2 miai

C[KyuT: As far as I could read, if you play F6 then I have to J5 (then E3,D2) and it's a carpenters square except I have the bonus of there being an atari on your F3 stones if they run out of liberties so I believe I win. If it goes F6,F4 then you win - (F6,F4,G7,H7,H8,G8,J7,G7,J5) then whatever I do as long as you answer D9 with C9 you win by either a liberty or a ko threat.
I felt the need to write all this down because I spent soooo long trying to read it out last night :)
